verificationNote: AI Generated: Customer information risk assessment methodology aligned to BNM MCIPD requirements. Organisations should adapt to their specific risk appetite and board-approved frameworks.
title: Customer Information Risk Assessment Methodology
description: Structured methodology for assessing customer information risks in Malaysian financial service providers, aligned to BNM Management of Customer Information and Permitted Disclosures (MCIPD) policy document.
proportionalityPrinciple: The depth, frequency, and rigour of the risk assessment must be proportionate to the FSP's size, nature, and complexity of operations, as well as the volume and sensitivity of customer information held (clause 10.4).

levels.label: Rare
levels.description: May occur only in exceptional circumstances (<5% annual probability)
levels.indicators: No history of occurrence; strong controls in place; threat actors have low motivation

levels.label: Unlikely
levels.description: Could occur but not expected (5-20% annual probability)
levels.indicators: Limited history of occurrence; controls are generally effective; external threat landscape is moderate

levels.label: Possible
levels.description: Might occur at some time (20-50% annual probability)
levels.indicators: Has occurred in the industry; some control weaknesses known; moderate threat actor capability and motivation

levels.label: Likely
levels.description: Will probably occur in most circumstances (50-80% annual probability)
levels.indicators: Has occurred at the FSP or frequently in the industry; known control gaps; active threat landscape

levels.label: Almost Certain
levels.description: Expected to occur in most circumstances (>80% annual probability)
levels.indicators: Regular occurrence; inadequate controls; high threat actor motivation and capability; ongoing vulnerabilities

levels.label: Insignificant
levels.description: Minimal impact on customers, no regulatory consequence, negligible financial loss
examples: Exposure of non-sensitive customer contact details to internal staff
Single customer affected with no financial or identity risk
Self-contained incident with no external exposure

levels.label: Minor
levels.description: Limited customer inconvenience, internal escalation, contained financial loss
examples: Misdirected customer correspondence to wrong address
Temporary unauthorised view of customer records by staff (no download)
Small number of customers affected with low-sensitivity data
levels.customerImpact: Minor inconvenience, low risk of harm
levels.regulatoryConsequence: Internal reporting, no BNM notification required

levels.label: Moderate
levels.description: Significant customer concern, potential BNM notification, material financial exposure
examples: Bulk customer data exposure (under 1,000 customers) with moderate sensitivity
Unauthorised download of customer records by staff
OSP control failure exposing customer data
levels.customerImpact: Significant concern, potential for identity risk

levels.label: Major
levels.description: Serious customer harm, BNM notification required, substantial financial and reputational impact
examples: Breach affecting over 1,000 customers (significant scale per clause 11.11)
Exposure of sensitive customer information (credentials, biometrics, medical data)
Customer data sold or disclosed to criminal elements
High-profile customer or public figure data exposed
levels.customerImpact: Significant harm including financial loss, identity theft risk, or safety threat
levels.regulatoryConsequence: BNM notification required, investigation report to Board and BNM, potential enforcement action

levels.label: Critical
levels.description: Catastrophic customer and institutional impact, licence-threatening, systemic implications
examples: Mass data breach affecting tens of thousands of customers
Customer data breach leading to widespread fraud or identity theft
Breach attracting national media attention and public confidence erosion
Deliberate large-scale data exfiltration by insider or external attacker
levels.customerImpact: Severe widespread harm, mass financial loss, or safety threats to customers
levels.regulatoryConsequence: Immediate BNM escalation, potential enforcement, licence review, law enforcement referral

bands.label: Low
bands.action: Accept and monitor through regular review cycle

bands.label: Medium
bands.action: Mitigate within 90 days; include in management reporting

bands.label: High
bands.action: Mitigate within 30 days; escalate to designated officer under S9.2

bands.label: Critical
bands.action: Immediate escalation to Board; mitigate within 14 days; notify BNM if threshold criteria met

steps.name: Asset Identification
steps.description: Identify and classify all customer information assets across systems, media, and locations

steps.name: Threat Identification
steps.description: Identify internal and external threats including negligent and deliberate acts

steps.name: Vulnerability Assessment
steps.description: Identify vulnerabilities in systems, processes, and people controls

steps.name: Risk Analysis
steps.description: Assess likelihood and impact for each threat-vulnerability pair, including third-party risks

steps.name: Risk Evaluation
steps.description: Compare risk levels against risk appetite and determine treatment priority

steps.name: Mitigation Planning
steps.description: Develop and implement mitigation plans based on identified risks

steps.name: Monitoring and Review
steps.description: Continuously monitor risk indicators and review assessment periodically

reviewCycle: Annually, or upon material change in operating environment, business model, or regulatory requirements
approvalAuthority: Designated officer under paragraph 9.2, with Board oversight under paragraph 8.3
